name: High-dose nicotinamide riboside did not improve skeletal-muscle mitochondrial endpoints
summary: A 12-week randomized trial in obese insulin-resistant men is a key boundary condition for NR mitochondrial claims.
statement: In middle-aged obese insulin-resistant men, 2 g/day nicotinamide riboside for 12 weeks did not increase skeletal-muscle NAD+ concentrations or improve mitochondrial respiration, mitochondrial content, or mitochondrial morphology.
quantitative_note: Forty participants received NR or placebo; muscle biopsies were used for respiration, protein, mRNA, and imaging endpoints.
caveats: The population was obese and insulin-resistant rather than healthy older adults.
The trial tested NR, not all NAD precursors.
The result is still a direct human skeletal-muscle boundary for broad NAD-mitochondria claims.
